vulnerabilities.title: The `size` option isn't honored after following a redirect in node-fetch
vulnerabilities.description:
### Impact
Node Fetch did not honor the `size` option after following a redirect, which means that when a content size was over the limit, a `FetchError` would never get thrown and the process would end without failure.

For most people, this fix will have a little or no impact. However, if you are relying on node-fetch to gate files above a size, the impact could be significant, for example: If you don't double-check the size of the data after `fetch()` has completed, your JS thread could get t

vulnerabilities.title: node-fetch forwards secure headers to untrusted sites
vulnerabilities.description: node-fetch forwards secure headers such as `authorization`, `www-authenticate`, `cookie`, & `cookie2` when redirecting to a untrusted site.

vulnerabilities.title: node-fetch Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity
vulnerabilities.description:
[node-fetch](https://www.npmjs.com/package/node-fetch) is a light-weight module that brings window.fetch to node.js.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in the `isOriginPotentiallyTrustworthy()` function in `referrer.js`, when processing a URL string with alternating letters and periods, such as `'http://' + 'a.a.'.repeat(i) + 'a'`.
